methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Pixxel is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $95M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 3 same-stage private companies in the sector: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $95M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $180.5M. The peer-median approach anchors to the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, which have a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $400M–$2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the public sector median trading multiple is 64.2x on P/S, but Pixxel's revenue is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $153M to $1.5B, with a median of $740M. The wide spread reflects material divergence between the conservative stage-progression method and the peer-median approach. Key uncertainties include the relative applicability of early-stage versus mid-stage peer valuations, the maturity and revenue trajectory implied by the $1.3B peer median, and the extent to which Pixxel's technology differentiation or customer traction justifies positioning toward the upper or lower end of the range.
